goal: Generate 10k COCO apple-detection images where annotation IDs stay stable across randomization steps.
thoughts: Replicator assigns category IDs per-step from the current semantic label set, so a class seen only in some steps gets a different ID. Stable IDs come from a writer that enforces a pre-declared class_name->category_id map, OR a post-run remap pass. Stay in CV vocabulary — Priya doesn't care about USD prims. The RTX speedup answer is path-tracing vs real-time profile, not disabling ray tracing.
code:
# 1. Configure COCO writer with a pinned class->id map
configure_coco_yolo_writer(
    output_dir="<DEST_PATH>/apples_coco",
    format="coco",
    class_id_map={"apple": 1, "leaf": 2, "branch": 3},  # stable across all steps
    include_instance_seg=True,
    include_semantic_seg=True,
)

# 2. Wire into the randomization pipeline
pipe = create_sdg_pipeline(
    name="apples_10k",
    randomizers=["lighting", "camera_pose"],
    writer="coco_pinned",
    num_frames=10000,
    seed=<SEED>,
)

# 3. Preview 8 frames to confirm IDs don't drift
preview = preview_sdg(pipeline=pipe.id, frame_count=8)
# Parent should eyeball the _annotations.coco.json category ids are [1,2,3] in every file

# 4. If built-in writer lacks pinning, fall back to remap pass
lookup_knowledge(query="omni replicator custom BasicWriter subclass class_id_map stable coco")
failure_modes: assistant lectures about USD semantic API instead of writer fix
assistant proposes disabling RTX entirely instead of profile swap
assistant ignores the instance vs semantic mask distinction when explaining leaf holes
